target.name: Neuralink

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $29.3B - $300.0B.

private.name: OpenAI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $300B+

private.name: Anthropic
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $183B

private.name: Anysphere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Private), post-money $29.3B

data.narrative:
Neuralink, a private-stage AI & Software company founded by Elon Musk and eight co-founders, has raised $1.3B+ and carries a current valuation of $9.7B. The comparable universe consists of three primary same-sector, same-stage peers: OpenAI ($300.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), and Anysphere ($29.3B), with an extended comp set including Palantir and xAI among private firms and Palantir (PLTR) as a public reference point.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given data constraints, yields a valuation of $183.0B based on the three-company peer set. This method implies a valuation range of $29.3B to $300.0B across the comparable universe. The peer-median approach reflects mid-point positioning within the AI & Software private equity market at this stage.

Triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $155.6B (low) to $210.4B (high), centering on $183.0B. The substantial gap between Neuralink's current $9.7B valuation and this derived range reflects either significant valuation compression relative to peer multiples or material differences in development stage, commercialization timeline, or investor risk assessment that the peer set does not fully capture. Key uncertainties include the applicability of pure software/AI comparables to a hardware-integrated neurotechnology platform and the timing and regulatory pathway to revenue generation, which may not be reflected in peer valuations anchored to software delivery models.
